steps.title: No solution means parallel lines
steps.content: Two equations have no solution when they represent parallel lines (same slope, different y-intercepts).

steps.title: Simplify to identify the slope pattern
steps.content: Divide the given equation by 2:$$x = 10y - 4$$For no solution, the second equation must have the form $x = 10y + c$ where $c \neq -4$.

steps.title: Check which option gives a parallel line
steps.content: Option B: $\frac{1}{2}x = 5y$. Multiply by 2:$$x = 10y$$This has form $x = 10y + 0$. Same slope as the first equation ($x = 10y - 4$) but different constant term, so the lines are parallel with no solution. That matches choice B — B is correct.
